title: GitHub adds review controls for agents that change issues
friendlyTitle: GitHub makes it easier to check an AI agent’s issue changes
summary: GitHub Issues can now attach confidence, rationale and optional approvals to agent-made changes such as labels, assignments, fields and closures. Repository administrators can choose which confidence levels apply automatically and which wait for review.
friendlySummary: AI agents can explain why they want to change a GitHub issue and say how confident they are. Teams can review uncertain changes before accepting them.
whyItMatters: Teams can automate routine triage without making every uncertain agent decision invisible or immediate. GitHub cautions that approvals are a workflow convenience rather than a hard security boundary, so agent permissions still need careful control.
friendlyWhyItMatters: This can save time while keeping people involved in decisions that may be wrong. Teams still need to limit what each agent is allowed to do.
